violations.text: Deli meats observed not marked consistently with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. All deli meats (including the most popular and fastest selling) must be marked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
The 3 bay sink was not in good repair, impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2024

Raw animal foods (such as beef) were observed stored on top of ready-to-eat foods Boston salads in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives in knife rack in walk in cooler, and knives in a knife storage bin) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024

Deli meats observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2024

Assorted salads, assorted grinders, wraps, buffalo chicken, stuffies, sausage, weiners on buns with toppings packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Items without complete must be removed from self service cases. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024

Clean work shirts observed stored above the mop sink. Items must be stored in a clean dry location, where they are not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the deli area. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024
The 3 bay sink was not in good repair, impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink and sanitizer bucket. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2024

The floors have an accumulation of soil residue and/or debris especially beneath market shelves. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024
